name: Turmeric
how: Mix with water to make a thick paste. Apply directly to wound. For fever, mix half teaspoon in warm water and drink.
found: Every Indian kitchen. Yellow powder or fresh root in markets.
warning: Do not apply to deep puncture wounds. Stains skin yellow temporarily.

name: Neem
how: Boil 10-15 neem leaves in 2 cups water for 10 minutes. Strain and drink warm for fever. Apply cooled liquid to skin rash.
found: Roadside trees all over Andhra Pradesh. Distinctive smell, small oval leaves.
warning: Very bitter. Small doses only — max 1 cup per day. Avoid during pregnancy.

name: Ginger
how: Cut 5-6 slices of fresh ginger. Boil in 2 cups water for 10 minutes. Add a little salt. Drink slowly. Repeat every 4 hours.
warning: Avoid if person has stomach ulcer or is on blood thinners.

name: Tulsi
how: Crush 10-15 fresh leaves. Boil in 1 cup water for 5 minutes. Add honey if available. Drink warm every 4-6 hours.
found: Most Hindu homes have a tulsi plant in the courtyard. Small green aromatic leaves.
warning: Generally very safe. Avoid very large amounts during pregnancy.

name: Aloe Vera
how: Break a thick leaf. Scoop out the clear gel inside. Apply directly to burn or wound. Cover loosely. Reapply every few hours.
found: Common in gardens and roadsides. Thick fleshy pointed green leaves.
warning: Only use the clear gel — the yellow liquid near the skin is a laxative. Do not eat in large amounts.

name: Garlic
how: Crush 2-3 raw garlic cloves. Apply paste to infected wound for 20 minutes then rinse. For internal: swallow 2 crushed cloves with water like a pill.
found: Every kitchen. White cloves with papery skin.
warning: Can cause skin irritation if left too long. May thin blood — avoid before surgery.

name: Coconut Water
how: Drink directly from young green coconut. If not available, mix 1L water + 6 teaspoons sugar + half teaspoon salt as emergency oral rehydration.
found: Coconut trees everywhere in AP. Also sold at roadside stalls.
warning: Avoid if person has kidney disease. Not a replacement for medical treatment in severe dehydration.

name: Honey
how: Apply raw honey directly to wound or burn as a dressing. Cover with clean cloth. Change every 12 hours. For cough: eat 1 teaspoon directly or mix in warm water.
found: Shops and markets. Use raw/natural honey, not processed.
warning: NEVER give honey to children under 1 year — risk of botulism. Do not use on deep wounds.

name: Mint
how: Crush fresh leaves, apply to forehead for headache. Boil in water and drink for nausea and stomach pain. Add to water to make it easier to drink during dehydration.
found: Common garden plant. Found in most Indian homes. Strong distinct smell.
warning: Generally very safe. Large amounts may cause heartburn.

name: Onion
how: Cut raw onion and rub on insect sting for immediate sting relief. Warm a slice and place on ear for ear pain. Inhale cut onion for nasal congestion.
warning: Skin irritant — do not leave on skin too long. May sting initially.

name: Tamarind
how: Soak tamarind pulp in water for 10 minutes. Squeeze out the liquid. Drink for digestive issues and mild fever cooling.
found: Every kitchen and market. Dark brown sticky pulp.
warning: High acidity — avoid in large amounts if stomach is sensitive. Avoid with certain medications.

name: Drumstick Leaves
how: Cook like spinach or boil in water and drink as a nutritious broth. Extremely high in vitamins and protein. Give to weak or malnourished survivors daily.
found: Drumstick trees common across Andhra Pradesh. Thin compound leaves.
warning: Safe in food amounts. Avoid root bark — it can be toxic.

name: Lemon / Lime
how: Mix juice of 1 lemon in 1L water with a pinch of salt and 2 teaspoons sugar — emergency ORS (Oral Rehydration Solution). Use juice to clean minor wounds.
warning: Do not use on deep wounds — causes burning. Dilute for sensitive stomachs.

name: Fenugreek
how: Soak 1 teaspoon seeds overnight in water. Drink the water in the morning. For fever: boil seeds in water and drink like tea.
found: Every kitchen spice box. Small yellow-brown seeds.
warning: May lower blood sugar — caution in diabetics on medication. Avoid large amounts in pregnancy.

name: Coriander Seeds
how: Boil 2 teaspoons seeds in 2 cups water for 10 minutes. Strain and drink throughout the day. Cooling effect on body — good for high fever.
warning: Generally very safe. Large amounts may cause skin sensitivity to sunlight.
